steps.annotation: Declare a string to hold the input
Prompt the user (no endl - cursor stays on same line)
cin pauses, waits for user to type, stores typed text in name
Declare int to receive the age value
cin reads from terminal stdin into age

levelLabel: L03 -> cin works for any type

steps.annotation: cin can read into int, double, string - matches the variable type

clozeSentence: Standard input stream is ___
explanation: cin reads from terminal stdin. It pauses and waits for the user to type.

clozeSentence: To read user input, use ___
explanation: cin gets data from the keyboard / terminal stdin.

clozeSentence: Read what the user typed into name with ___
explanation: cin reads from stdin into the variable on the right of >>.

clozeSentence: Read input into the variable named ___
explanation: cin >> stores the typed input into the variable that follows.

spec: Write a single statement to read a string from the terminal into a variable named name.
expectedAnswer: cin >> name;
explanation: cin >> reads the next token from stdin into name.

spec: Write code that declares a string username, then reads it from the terminal.
expectedAnswer:
string username;
cin >> username;
explanation: Declare the variable, then use cin to fill it.

spec: Write code that declares an int age and reads it from the terminal.
expectedAnswer:
int age;
cin >> age;
explanation: Declare int variable, then read into it with cin >>.

spec: Write a complete program that reads a name from the terminal and prints Hello name.
explanation: Declare string, read with cin, then output greeting using cout.

spec: Write a complete program that reads an int age and prints the age back.
explanation: Standard read-then-print pattern using cin and cout.

question: Type the standard cin call to read into variable n

steps.annotation: First >> stores typed input into a
Second >> stores next typed input into b
Use the values that >> placed into a and b
Arrow >> points away from cin TOWARD word - data flows that direction

clozeSentence: The input operator is ___
explanation: >> is the input operator. It reads from cin into the variable on its right.

clozeSentence: Read into the variable named ___
explanation: >> stores the input into the variable on its right side.

clozeSentence: The value comes from typed input via the ___ stream
explanation: cin is the input source. >> moves a value from cin into the variable.

clozeSentence: Reuse the same operator ___ to read another value
explanation: >> reads each value. You can repeat it for each variable.

spec: Write a single statement to read a double named price from terminal input.
expectedAnswer: cin >> price;
explanation: >> reads the next token from cin into price.

spec: Write two separate statements to read int a then int b from the terminal (no chaining).
expectedAnswer:
cin >> a;
cin >> b;
explanation: Two separate cin calls, each using >> to read one value.

spec: Write code that declares string firstName then reads input into it.
expectedAnswer:
string firstName;
cin >> firstName;
explanation: >> reads into firstName from terminal stdin.

spec: Write a complete program that reads two int values a and b and prints their sum.
explanation: Two cin reads, then cout prints the sum.

spec: Write a complete program that reads a string color and prints it back.
explanation: Read string from input, echo with cout.

prompt: Write the cin statement to read into int variable score.
expectedAnswer: cin >> score;

variants.prompt: Write the cin statement to read into double variable temperature.
Write the cin statement to read into string variable city.

steps.annotation: Program PAUSES here until user types a value and presses Enter
Only runs after user has typed something for a
PAUSES again, waits for second value
Even after typing a number, program waits for Enter to release

clozeSentence: What pauses the program for input is ___
explanation: cin >> blocks the program until the user provides input.

code:
cin ___ value;
// pauses until Enter pressed
clozeSentence: The input operator that triggers waiting is ___
explanation: cin >> blocks. Without >> nothing actually triggers a read.

clozeSentence: Read into the variable named ___
explanation: cin >> pin pauses the program until pin gets a value from stdin.

code:
// reads into k, blocks for input
cin ___ k;
clozeSentence: Use ___ between cin and k
explanation: cin >> k blocks until user enters a value for k.

spec: Write code that prompts Enter PIN: then reads an int pin and echoes it back.
explanation: Prompt with cout (no endl), read with cin, echo with cout.

spec: Write code that reads an int code from terminal then prints code received.
explanation: cin >> code blocks until user types. Then the cout runs.

spec: Write code that reads a string command and an int count separately (two cin calls).
explanation: Each cin call blocks for one value. Two calls = two input pauses.

spec: Write a complete program that prompts Enter line one: then reads a string s, then prompts Enter line two: then reads another string t, then prints s and t on one line.
explanation: Two prompts, two reads, then print both with a space separator.

spec: Write a complete program that reads an int n and prints n squared (n times n).
explanation: Read with cin, compute n*n, print with cout.

question: Type the canonical pause-for-input statement that reads int x

steps.annotation: Reads up to first whitespace - reads 3
Skips space, reads next token - 7

levelLabel: L03 -> spaces or newlines both work

steps.annotation: Reads next non-whitespace token - 4
Skips any whitespace (space, tab, newline), reads 5

clozeSentence: Values are separated by ___ in the input
explanation: Whitespace (space/tab/newline) separates input tokens. Either a space or newline between 5 and 10 works.

clozeSentence: The operator that splits at whitespace is ___
explanation: >> reads a single whitespace-delimited token from the input stream.

clozeSentence: After cin >>, only the first word goes into ___
explanation: cin >> reads up to whitespace. So 'hello world' fills firstWord with 'hello'.

clozeSentence: x receives the value ___
explanation: cin >> reads up to whitespace. The first token 12 fills x; 34 stays in the buffer for the next read.

spec: Write code to read two ints x and y from one line of input separated by space.
expectedAnswer:
cin >> x;
cin >> y;
explanation: Two separate cin >> calls. cin >> automatically skips whitespace between tokens.

spec: Write code that declares string colorA and string colorB then reads two color words from input separated by whitespace.
explanation: Two reads. Whitespace between colors splits them into separate tokens.

spec: Write code that reads three ints a, b, c using one cin call per variable.
explanation: Three reads. Each >> skips whitespace and grabs one token.

spec: Write code that prompts Enter two numbers: then reads int p then int q.
explanation: Prompt once, then read two whitespace-separated ints.

spec: Write a complete program that reads two ints from one line and prints their product.
explanation: Two cin calls split on whitespace. Then product printed.

steps.annotation: score is int - cin parses digits 85 as integer
grade is string - cin reads next word A
double type - cin reads digits including decimal point

clozeSentence: cin parses '42' as int because the variable is int. Read into ___
explanation: cin >> matches the input format to the variable's type. count is int so 42 parses as int.

clozeSentence: To read a word into a string, declare type ___
explanation: string variable + cin >> reads one whitespace-delimited word.

clozeSentence: To read a decimal value, declare type ___
explanation: double holds floating-point numbers. cin >> parses 9.8 according to double's format.

clozeSentence: For numeric input, declare ___
explanation: int matches integer input format. cin >> reads digits into the int variable.

spec: Write code that reads a double value into a variable named height.
expectedAnswer:
double height;
cin >> height;
explanation: double type - cin reads decimal-format numbers into it.

spec: Write code that reads an int year and a string month from terminal.
explanation: Different types - cin parses each according to declared type.

spec: Write code that reads a double weight and an int reps from terminal.
explanation: Mixed types each read with cin >>; parsing matches declared variable type.

spec: Write a complete program that reads a string username and an int userId then prints them on separate lines.
explanation: Different types - cin parses each accordingly.

spec: Write a complete program that reads a double temperature and prints it back.
explanation: double type. cin reads decimal input into temperature.

prompt: Write the declaration and read for a string named city.
expectedAnswer:
string city;
cin >> city;

question: Type the canonical 2-line pattern: declare int age then read it

steps.annotation: Single statement reads three values - same as 3 separate cin calls
All three values now hold what user typed

levelLabel: L03 -> chain reads name then age

steps.annotation: Reads next word into name, then next token into age

code:
int x, y;
cin >> x ___ y;
clozeSentence: Chain reads using ___
explanation: Repeated >> chains reads. cin >> x >> y reads two values in one statement.

code:
int a, b, c;
cin >> a >> ___ >> c;
clozeSentence: Read three values with chained >>: a, ___, c
explanation: Chain reads variable by variable. a then b then c.

clozeSentence: Chain begins with the input stream ___
explanation: The chain starts at cin. cin >> first >> last reads two words.

clozeSentence: Read p first then q - fill in ___
explanation: cin >> p >> q reads p first, then q.

spec: Write a single chained cin statement that reads two ints x and y.
expectedAnswer: cin >> x >> y;
explanation: Chain >> in one statement to read multiple values.

spec: Write a chained cin statement that reads three values: int a, double b, string c.
expectedAnswer: cin >> a >> b >> c;
explanation: One statement, three reads via chained >>.

spec: Write code that declares int hours and int minutes then reads both with one chained cin.
explanation: Declare both ints, then chain >> to read both in one statement.

spec: Write code that reads a string firstName, string lastName, and int age all in one chained cin.
explanation: Three declarations, one chained read.

spec: Write a complete program that reads three ints in one chained cin and prints their sum.
explanation: Chained read into 3 vars, then sum and print.

prompt: Write a chained cin to read int width and int height.
expectedAnswer: cin >> width >> height;

variants.prompt: Write a chained cin to read string name and int age.
Write a chained cin to read double x, double y, double z.

examples.label: Example A: 2 ints
examples.code:
int a, b;
cin >> a >> b;

examples.label: Example B: 3 ints
examples.code:
int a, b, c;
cin >> a >> b >> c;

prompt: Write the declaration and chained cin for FOUR ints: a, b, c, d.
expectedAnswer:
int a, b, c, d;
cin >> a >> b >> c >> d;

steps.annotation: Reads everything until Enter - INCLUDING spaces - into fullLine
Print the entire line back

levelLabel: L03 -> getline vs cin >> for sentences

steps.annotation: cin >> would only read 'hello'. getline reads the full 'hello world' including space

clozeSentence: Read a whole line including spaces using ___
explanation: getline(cin, s) reads up to newline - keeps internal spaces.

clozeSentence: First argument to getline is the input stream ___
explanation: getline(cin, var) - cin is the source of input.

clozeSentence: getline reads into the second argument variable ___
explanation: Second argument is the destination string.

clozeSentence: To read a sentence with spaces use ___
explanation: getline preserves embedded whitespace; cin >> would stop at the first space.

spec: Write a single getline call that reads into a string named line.
expectedAnswer: getline(cin, line);
explanation: getline(cin, line) reads a full line into the string.

spec: Write code that declares string address then uses getline to read it.
expectedAnswer:
string address;
getline(cin, address);
explanation: getline reads into address - keeps spaces in the address.

spec: Write code that prompts Enter full name: then reads an entire line with getline into string fullName.
explanation: Prompt with cout, then getline reads full line with spaces.

spec: Write a complete program that uses getline to read a string sentence and prints it back.
explanation: Read full line with getline, echo with cout.

spec: Write a complete program that uses getline to read a string question and prints It said: question.
explanation: getline grabs the whole line including any spaces in the question.

prompt: Write the declaration and getline for a variable named comment of type string.
expectedAnswer:
string comment;
getline(cin, comment);

question: Type the canonical getline call to read into string line

steps.annotation: Show prompt without endl - cursor stays on same line
User types value next to prompt - cin reads it into qty

steps.annotation: Whitespace splits the input into x and y
Echo input plus computed sum

prompt: Write declaration and read for int variable count.
expectedAnswer:
int count;
cin >> count;
